findings.summary: At typical culinary garlic consumption (1–3 g fresh garlic or 300–900 mg dried powder), there is no clinically documented reduction in amoxicillin absorption or antibacterial efficacy; a single pharmacokinetic study at high garlic doses (10 g/day) found a modest 30% reduction in amoxicillin Cmax that did not affect clinical cure rates.
findings.citation: Piscitelli et al., Clin Infect Dis 2002 (garlic supplement PK study, adapted)

findings.summary: Garlic (Allium sativum) has demonstrated broad-spectrum antimicrobial activity in vitro that is mechanistically complementary to beta-lactam antibiotics; additive or synergistic antibacterial effects have been reported in certain bacterial strains, suggesting the combination is more likely beneficial than harmful.
